title: ferray-numpy-interop: no null handling strategy — nulls always rejected
description:
Goal: 

Acceptance criteria:
- 

All From* conversions reject null values: 'ferray arrays do not support nulls'. This is correct (ferray has no null concept) but there's no strategy for handling nullable data, which is ubiquitous in Arrow and Polars. Options: (1) convert to ferray MaskedArray where nulls become mask=true, (2) accept a fill_value parameter that replaces nulls, (3) convert to ferray array + separate bool validity array. NumPy's masked_array serves exactly this purpose. At minimum provide a from_arrow_with_fill(arr, fill_value) or from_arrow_masked(arr) -> MaskedArray.
